description: Keep loop child sessions on fresh session history while inheriting the parent session's resolved <memory> view for stable multi-iteration behavior.

userStories.title: Persist resolved memory payload per session
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one session-linked place to store the resolved memory payload so loop-created child sessions can inherit the exact rendered memory view.
acceptanceCriteria: Add one session-linked storage path for a session's rendered <memory> block, rendered memory conflict block, and deterministic empty-memory state
Store the final rendered text shown to the model, not only memory IDs or recall metadata
Keep existing non-loop sessions functional after the storage path is added
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Capture rendered memory during injection
userStories.description: As a loop system, I want memory injection to save the resolved payload for the current session so later loop children can reuse it without recomputing on their first turn.
acceptanceCriteria: When memory injection adds a <memory> block, save the exact rendered block to the session-linked storage path
When memory injection adds a memory conflict block, save the exact rendered conflict block to the same session-linked storage path
When no memory is injected, save the explicit empty-memory state selected for this feature
Existing memory injection output for normal sessions remains unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Inherit resolved memory for loop child first turn
userStories.description: As a loop-created child session, I want to reuse the parent session's resolved memory payload on my first LLM turn so memory context stays stable across /ralph-loop and /ultrawork iterations.
acceptanceCriteria: When /ralph-loop creates a child session, copy the parent session's latest resolved memory payload into the child session before the child's first LLM turn
When /ultrawork creates a child session, copy the parent session's latest resolved memory payload into the child session before the child's first LLM turn
The child's first LLM turn uses the inherited rendered memory payload instead of recomputing memory injection
Loop child sessions still do not inherit parent message history
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Refresh inherited memory only on normal triggers
userStories.description: As the memory system, I want loop child sessions to keep inherited memory until existing dirty or re-recall rules require recomputation so continuity improves without inventing a second refresh policy.
acceptanceCriteria: After the first child turn, inherited rendered memory remains active across child turns until dirty-state or re-recall threshold rules require recomputation
If recomputation happens, save the child's newly resolved rendered memory payload for future descendants
If the parent session has no resolved memory payload, the child falls back to the normal memory injection flow on its first turn
Non-loop sessions continue using the current memory refresh behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Preserve loop scope boundaries
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want loop memory inheritance to stay narrowly scoped so only later loop-created child sessions change behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The initial session where /ralph-loop or /ultrawork is invoked keeps its current behavior
Oracle verification sessions keep their current behavior and do not inherit parent resolved memory in this feature
Task tool, subagent-created child sessions, manual forks, and unrelated child-session flows keep their current behavior
No task-local work summary is copied into <memory> as part of this feature
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add regression tests for loop memory inheritance
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated coverage for loop memory inheritance so later refactors do not silently reintroduce recomputation or scope creep.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test coverage for saving rendered <memory> and conflict payloads on a parent session
Add test coverage for /ralph-loop child first-turn inheritance
Add test coverage for /ultrawork child first-turn inheritance
Add test coverage showing loop child sessions do not inherit parent message history
Add test coverage for first-turn fallback to normal injection when the parent has no resolved payload
Add test coverage showing initial invocation sessions and Oracle verification sessions keep current behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
